comments.body:
> Really nifty work overall @cevich. I'm just shocked you've any hair left after this process...

Lol thanks, believe me, it's starting to thin :disappointed: Hopefully it will all work out, and maybe, just maybe, after some runtime battle-testing, we can switch to using bors-ng in our other repos.
This PR was included in a batch that was canceled, it will be automatically retried
This PR was included in a batch that timed out, it will be automatically retried
Updated with screen-shot showing how to interact with a bors-ng 'try' or 'merge' in progress.
(That failure was my doing, travis was doing bad things.  We need to wait for #2115 to merge, then bors will automatically retry and it should work)
:-1: Rejected by too few approved reviews
:-1: Rejected by too few approved reviews
Going to add @baude as an fyi

Woopsie.  @TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan I misinterpreted a setting - fixed it and the documentation in the most recent commit.

Saying 'bors r+' alone should work now (no github reviewers required) - unless you want them to be required, then I can set it back to `1` :grin:
:clock1: Waiting for PR status (Github check) to be set, probably by CI. Bors will automatically try to run when all required PR statuses are set.
Bors does not like me today.  Forcing merge.

> Waiting for PR status (Github check) to be set, probably by CI. Bors will automatically try to run when all required PR statuses are set.

Hrmmm, not sure about that one.  I'll need to check on the forum if it keeps happening.
